description: build_snapshot currently errors on non-core namespaces because StoreRuntime only has CanonicalState. Extend snapshot capture once StoreState is plumbed so checkpoint groups can include multiple namespaces.
title: Checkpoint snapshot builder rejects non-core namespaces

description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §0.3/§2.7 require StoreState (namespaced CanonicalState) + namespace-aware access. Current implementation remains single-namespace: RepoState holds CanonicalState, and IPC normalization rejects any namespace other than core. This means namespaces.toml policies are loaded but never used, and non-core namespaces cannot be created or queried.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/core.rs:1865 normalize_namespace() returns NamespaceUnknown for any non-core.
- src/daemon/repo.rs: RepoState.state is CanonicalState (not StoreState).
- src/daemon/executor.rs and src/daemon/query_executor.rs apply/query against repo_state.state directly.

**Why this violates plan**
REALTIME_PLAN mandates StoreState = map<NamespaceId, CanonicalState> and a defaulting rule (namespace=core) rather than hard-rejecting non-core. This is a core architectural requirement.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Replace RepoState.state with StoreState; thread namespace selection through executor, query, replication ingest, checkpoint export/import.
- [ ] normalize_namespace validates against store.policies (namespaces.toml) and allows non-core namespaces when configured.
- [ ] Defaulting rule remains: missing namespace -> core.
- [ ] Unit tests cover non-core namespace mutation + query + replication paths.
title: Namespaces still hard-coded to core; StoreState not wired

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Decoding a namespace list always yields canonical (sorted + deduped) order.
- [ ] Handshake replay accepts the same set regardless of wire ordering.
- [ ] Internal storage uses the canonical type (no raw Vec in session peer).
- [ ] Encoding always emits canonical order.
- [ ] Tests cover: same set different order, duplicates on wire, empty list.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes.
description:
**Problem**
Replication wire decoding returns raw `Vec<NamespaceId>` for `requested_namespaces`, `offered_namespaces`, and `accepted_namespaces`. Order is preserved from the wire, not canonicalized. Later we compare vectors for equality (e.g. WELCOME replay), which can reject a peer that sends the same set in a different order.

This is a parse-don’t-validate violation (we should normalize at the boundary), and a classic scatter bug: correctness depends on every caller remembering to sort/dedup.

**Impact**
- False handshake failures due to ordering differences.
- Hidden coupling to wire ordering; brittle and hard to reason about.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/proto.rs` (decode_namespace_list)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs` (HELLO/WELCOME replay comparison)
title: Repl namespace lists must be canonicalized at parse boundary

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `cargo test -p beads-daemon --lib` no longer fails in `testkit/e2e.rs` on trunk-equivalent code
- [ ] `testkit::e2e` only compiles when its supporting APIs are available under the same gate
- [ ] Existing `test-harness` lib/integration coverage still works
description:
**Problem**
`cargo test -p beads-daemon --lib` fails on `main@tip` before any tests run because `crates/beads-daemon/src/testkit/e2e.rs` is compiled under plain `cfg(test)` even though it imports APIs that are only available when the `test-harness` feature is enabled.

**Why It Matters**
This makes the non-canonical daemon lib-test path lie about the crate health and complicates verification for test-infrastructure work. The failure is pre-existing on trunk, so it needs an explicit tracker home instead of being hand-waved as local noise.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/testkit/mod.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/testkit/e2e.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/core/repo_access.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/core/repl_ingest.rs`
- `crates/beads-core/src/time.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/Cargo.toml`
design: Make the gating consistent so plain `cargo test -p beads-daemon --lib` either does not compile `testkit::e2e` without `test-harness`, or the APIs `e2e.rs` depends on are available under the same condition. The fix should preserve the existing `test-harness` workflow and remove the compile mismatch between `cfg(test)` and feature-gated helpers.

title: Gate beads-daemon testkit::e2e consistently with test-harness
